title: A67: Recursion — Tower of Hanoi
directions_html:
In this assignment, you will complete one function in main.hpp.
Do NOT edit main.cpp.

Tower of Hanoi

Move n disks from peg from to peg to using peg aux as a helper.

Rules:

  • Move only one disk at a time
  • Never place a larger disk on a smaller disk
Function: int hanoi(int n, char from, char to, char aux)

Use a static local variable to count recursive calls. Return the total count.

Recursive logic:

static int count = 0;
count++;                          // count this call
if (n == 1) return count;         // base case
hanoi(n-1, from, aux, to);        // move top n-1 to aux
hanoi(n-1, aux, to, from);        // move n-1 from aux to to
return count;

Call count by n:

nTotal callsFormula
112¹ - 1
232² - 1
372³ - 1
4152⁴ - 1

Each test runs in a separate process — the static count resets to 0 each time.

To compile and run:

g++ main.cpp
./a.out

To run tests:

make test
make test ARGS="[T1]"
elaborate_note: Why is the number of calls always 2ⁿ - 1? Explain how each recursive call spawns two more calls, and how the base case stops the recursion.
